egg: noun. An approximately spherical or ellipsoidal body produced by birds, insects, reptiles, and other animals, often housing an embryo
erg: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Sie.
ers: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ersu.
gel: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Fakkanci.
gest: noun. A story or adventure; a verse or prose romance.
gets: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of get
glue: noun. A hard gelatin made by boiling bones and hides, used in solution as an adhesive; or any sticky adhesive substance.
glug: noun. The sound made when a significant amount of liquid is poured suddenly out of something, such as a jug or bottle.
grue: verb. To be frightened; also, to shudder with fear; to quake, to tremble.
gruel: noun. A thin, watery porridge, formerly eaten primarily by the poor and the ill.
grues: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of grue
guest: noun. A recipient of hospitality, especially someone staying by invitation at the house of another.
gul: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gullah.
gules: noun. Red, e.g. on a coat of arms, typically represented in engraving by vertical parallel lines.
gurgle: verb. To flow with a bubbling sound.
gust: noun. A strong, abrupt rush of wind.
gut: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Maléku Jaíka.
leg: noun. A limb or appendage that an animal uses for support or locomotion on land.
lest: conjunction. For fear that; that not; in order to prevent something from happening; in case.
let: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lesing-Gelimi.
leu: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kara (New Guinea).
lug: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Luganda.
luge: noun. A racing sled for one or two people that is ridden with the rider or riders lying on their back.
luger: noun. Someone who competes in the luge.
lugger: noun. That which lugs in either literal or figurative senses.
lure: noun. Something that tempts or attracts, especially one with a promise of reward or pleasure.
lust: noun. A feeling of strong desire, especially such a feeling driven by sexual arousal.
luster: noun. The ability or condition of shining with reflected light; sheen, gleam, gloss, sparkle, shine, etc.
lustre: noun. UK standard spelling of luster (shine, etc.).
lute: noun. A fretted stringed instrument, similar to the guitar, having a bowl-shaped body or soundbox; any of a wide variety of chordophones
reg: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kara (Tanzania).
rest: noun. Relief from work or activity by sleeping; sleep.
result: verb. To proceed, spring up or rise, as a consequence, from facts, arguments, premises, combination of circumstances, consultation, thought
ret: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Retta.
rets: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of ret
rue: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Carpathian Rusyn.
rug: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Roviana.
ruse: noun. A turning or doubling back, especially of animals to get out of the way of hunting dogs.
rust: noun. The deteriorated state of iron or steel as a result of moisture and oxidation; it consists mostly of iron(III) oxide (ferric oxide)
rustle: noun. A soft crackling sound similar to the movement of dry leaves.
rut: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Rutul.
seg: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Segeju.
sel: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Selkup.
ser: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Serrano.
set: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Sentani.
slue: verb. To rotate something on an axis.
slug: noun. Any of many gastropod mollusks, having no (or only a rudimentary) shell.
slugger: noun. A boxer who tends to deliver hard punches.
slur: noun. An insult or slight, especially one that is muttered incoherently under one's breath.
struggle: noun. A contortion of the body in an attempt to escape or to perform a difficult task.
sue: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Suena.
suer: noun. Someone who sues; a suitor.
suet: noun. The fatty tissue that surrounds and protects the kidneys; that of sheep and cattle is used in cooking and in making tallow.
sure: adjective. Physically secure and certain, non-failing, reliable.
surge: noun. A sudden transient rush, flood or increase.
sutler: noun. A person who follows an army, selling provisions.
teg: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Teke-Tege.
tel: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Telugu.
true: adjective. Conforming to the actual state of reality or fact; factually correct.
trues: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of true
trug: noun. A shallow, oval basket used for gardening
tug: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tunia.
tugger: noun. Agent noun of tug; one who tugs.
tule: noun. Any of a number of large freshwater sedges of western North America formerly classified in the genus Scirpus, but now mostly as
ulster: noun. A long, loose overcoat made of wool or other rough material, often called a greatcoat, which sometimes features an attached shoulder
urge: noun. A strong desire to do something.
user: noun. One who uses or makes use of something, a consumer or client or an express or implied licensee (free user) or a trespasser.
